JPM.reasoning: Branch B fires — buy the dip in a strong uptrend. JPM's daily trend is unambiguously bullish (EMA stack aligned, ADX 43.42 confirming strong trend), but price has pulled back from the $320.24 swing high to test the 23.6% Fibonacci retracement at $310.82. The Apr 27 session printed a textbook hammer/reversal candle: open $306.88, bounce to $311.63 close, recovering ~1.5% intraday — a clear rejection of lower prices. Hourly RSI divergence is bullish (confirmed in data), signaling the reversal is already underway. Daily RSI-7 has compressed to 23.27 (deep short-term oversold), consistent with a healthy pullback washout in a trend. The volume-confirmed hourly support at $307.29 (14 tests) and the wiki-documented entry zone of $307.00–$307.50 align with the Apr 27 low of $306.81, providing strong S/R confluence. Hourly price has already reclaimed all major MAs (above EMA-24, SMA-24, EMA-48, SMA-48, EMA-120, SMA-120), and the hourly MACD histogram is positive (+0.14), confirming upside momentum resumption. Branch A does not fire: daily Z-score is only 1.0 (well within normal range), %B is 0.555 (near the middle band, not stretched), and 50-SMA distance is 3.64% (below the 5% threshold). No daily RSI divergence. The main risk is the daily MACD histogram turning negative (-0.29) and OBV diverging from price on the daily — suggesting upside may be capped near the $314.99–$315.45 resistance cluster rather than a full retest of $320.24. Confidence is moderate (62) given the daily MACD headwind and the fact that the 23.6% Fib is a relatively shallow pullback level. Target is the $314.99 hourly resistance (24 tests), stop below the $306.81 recent swing low / volume-confirmed $307.29 support. R/R: entry $309.50, target $314.99 (+$5.49), stop $306.50 (-$3.00) = 1.83:1 R/R, borderline but acceptable given trend strength.
